title: Design Circular Queue
pattern: Design â€” Fixed-size Array with Modular Pointers
coreIdea: Implement a fixed-capacity FIFO queue using an array where front and rear pointers wrap around using modular arithmetic, reusing freed space instead of shifting elements.
recognitionCues: asks to design a queue with a fixed maximum capacity
efficient space reuse (circular wraparound) is the expected mechanism
approach: Maintain an array of fixed size, a front index, a count of current elements (or a rear index with a full/empty flag to disambiguate). Enqueue: place at (front + count) % capacity, increment count. Dequeue: increment front (mod capacity), decrement count. Track count to distinguish full vs empty when front == rear.
